hypothesis: Incorporating electron beam dose rate as a dynamic conditioning variable during SAM-EM fine-tuning—by generating synthetic training data that explicitly couples beam-induced radiolytic artifact patterns (e.g., bubble formation, contrast drift) with corresponding dose rate metadata fed as an auxiliary input channel—will enable the model to disentangle beam-driven particle motion artifacts from true diffusive dynamics, yielding statistically distinguishable MSD curves that allow quantitative separation of beam-induced anomalous diffusion from intrinsic nanoparticle behavior across experimentally relevant dose rates.
